steps.title: Identify the hypothesis's core assumption
steps.content: The student inferred same egg-laying depth from identical pre-hatching temperatures (20–22°C). This reasoning assumes that identical temperatures in different regions correspond to identical depths.

steps.title: Test which finding breaks that assumption
steps.content:
Choice C — The water temperature at a given depth tends to be lower in waters near New Caledonia than it is at that depth in waters near Fiji. breaks this assumption by introducing regional temperature variation: if the same depth has different temperatures in the two regions, then identical temperatures across regions point to different depths.

The others don't target this logic:
  • A supports the hypothesis
  • B focuses on post-hatching mobility
  • D addresses suitability
.

That matches Choice C — The water temperature at a given depth tends to be lower in waters near New Caledonia than it is at that depth in waters near Fiji..
